description: This figure presents a schematic diagram illustrating the thermomechanical processing route for preparing cryogenically rolled Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C low-density steel specimens. The workflow originates from 100 mm × 100 mm billets that are sectioned into plate-shaped specimens with thicknesses of 1.5, 2.0, 3.5, and 10.0 mm. These plates undergo solution treatment at 1000 °C for 1 hour followed by water quenching, as depicted in the temperature-time profile. Subsequently, the specimens are subjected to a cyclic cryogenic rolling process: they are immersed in liquid nitrogen (the specific duration is not visible in the image), then rolled between working rolls, and immediately re-immersed. This circulation process is repeated until final cumulative thickness reductions of 30%, 50%, 70%, and 90% are achieved. The caption reports additional details including 3–5 minutes immersion per cycle, approximately 2% thickness reduction per pass, and the use of an LG-300 two-high rolling mill. This diagram establishes the experimental protocol for investigating microstructure evolution and mechanical property development.
